coreJobStatement: Operate and maintain a client's built environment to achieve continuous safety compliance, operational readiness, and regulatory conformance while minimising client-side labour
productJobStatement: Help Door & Exit Monitoring System to detect unauthorized emergency exit door activations and produce audit-ready event logs for FM compliance workflows -- without requiring wired infrastructure or on-site inspection rounds
lPath: L6e Safety & Compliance Management > L5 Door & Exit Monitoring System > L4 Door Guardian Device

segments.name: Portfolio FM operator
segments.characteristics: 20+ client sites across mixed building types; requires standardised devices deployable per-site and centralised multi-building dashboard
segments.targetabilityReason: Single-site solutions do not aggregate; wired solutions do not scale cost-effectively; portfolio dashboard is the decisive differentiator
segments.circumstance: 20+ client sites across mixed building types; requires standardised devices deployable per-site and centralised multi-building dashboard
segments.alternativesDiffer: Single-site solutions do not aggregate; wired solutions do not scale cost-effectively

segments.name: Compliance FM specialist
segments.characteristics: Inspection-only contracts; client demands digital, audit-proof evidence chains with no paper archives or manual log assembly
segments.targetabilityReason: Documentation capability becomes the competitive differentiator; IoT event logs directly satisfy this
segments.circumstance: Inspection-only contracts; client demands digital, audit-proof evidence chains with no paper archives or manual log assembly
segments.alternativesDiffer: Documentation capability becomes the competitive differentiator

segments.name: Single-site IFM contract
segments.characteristics: One large building (hospital campus, corporate HQ); deep BMS integration required; wired alternatives reduce integration friction
segments.targetabilityReason: Proprietary gateway friction (C6) tips decision toward wired for BMS-heavy sites
segments.circumstance: One large building (hospital campus, corporate HQ); deep BMS integration required; wired alternatives reduce integration friction
segments.alternativesDiffer: Deep BMS integration required; wired alternatives reduce integration friction

segments.name: Hard Services operator
segments.characteristics: MEP and fire safety maintenance only; device must be low-maintenance and high-reliability across diverse door hardware types
segments.targetabilityReason: Maintenance overhead per device is the primary total cost driver; low-maintenance wireless is valued
segments.circumstance: MEP and fire safety maintenance only; device must be low-maintenance and high-reliability across diverse door hardware types
segments.alternativesDiffer: Maintenance overhead per device is the primary total cost driver

alternatives.name: Hardwired electronic alarm systems (door contacts wired to fire panel)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Requires mains cabling and conduit in retrofit buildings; electrician sign-off; EUR 150-300 labour/door on top of hardware; single cable-fault can drop a circuit; asbestos risk in pre-1980 buildings

alternatives.name: Battery-powered wireless alarm devices (Turkwachter class, LoRaWAN door sensors)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Battery replacement cycles (1-3 yr); wireless range limits (~30-60 m); proprietary protocol friction (EasyWave != BACnet/KNX); higher unit cost than mechanical

alternatives.name: Mechanical-only door alarms (no monitoring, no wireless)
alternatives.tradeoffs: No event logging; no remote alerting; compliance evidence requires manual inspection; declining installed base; no portfolio visibility

alternatives.name: CCTV / video monitoring at exit points
alternatives.tradeoffs: No alarm-on-open; requires active human review; high infrastructure cost; GDPR data-retention complexity; no audit-grade timestamped event log

alternatives.name: Do nothing / manual inspection rounds [Status Quo]
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero P1 burden; FM inspection labor cost only (est. EUR 6-12/door/visit quarterly); compliance gap risk accepted

jobSteps.description: Define which door points require monitoring, alarm thresholds, and compliance reporting requirements per client building
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Scoping occurs at FM contract level before device selection; product does not influence this step
jobSteps.rawStatement: Define which door points require monitoring, alarm thresholds, and compliance reporting requirements per client building
jobSteps.rationale: Scoping occurs at FM contract level before device selection; product does not influence this step

jobSteps.description: Map and record all emergency exit door points and hardware specifications across client buildings
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Asset mapping precedes device deployment; product does not influence inventory creation
jobSteps.rawStatement: Map and record all emergency exit door points and hardware specifications across client buildings
jobSteps.rationale: Asset mapping precedes device deployment; product does not influence inventory creation

jobSteps.description: Install monitoring devices, configure gateways, integrate with client BMS/CMMS, and commission alarm triggers across all door points
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Device installation and commissioning is the core deployment task; installation method (surface-mount wireless vs. wired) directly determines FM labor cost
jobSteps.rawStatement: Install monitoring devices, configure gateways, integrate with client BMS/CMMS, and commission alarm triggers
jobSteps.rationale: Device installation and commissioning is the core deployment task; installation method (surface-mount wireless vs. wired) directly determines FM labor cost

jobSteps.description: Verify all monitoring devices trigger alarms within specification and that events reach the FM dashboard correctly before handover
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Post-commissioning verification tests device reliability; misconfiguration at this step is the leading cause of compliance failures discovered during fire marshal inspections
jobSteps.rawStatement: Verify all monitoring devices trigger alarms within specification and events reach the FM dashboard correctly
jobSteps.rationale: Post-commissioning verification tests device reliability; misconfiguration at this step is the leading cause of compliance failures discovered during fire marshal inspections

jobSteps.description: Detect unauthorized door activations in real time, trigger audible alarms, and log events timestamped to the FM monitoring platform
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Primary operational function of the device; detection latency and alarm reliability are the core performance KPIs
jobSteps.rawStatement: Detect unauthorized door activations in real time, trigger alarms, and log events to FM monitoring platform
jobSteps.rationale: Primary operational function of the device; detection latency and alarm reliability are the core performance KPIs

jobSteps.description: Track real-time device health -- battery levels, wireless signal integrity, and alarm frequency per door point -- across the FM portfolio
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: IoT reporting capability (battery alerts, signal health, dashboard coverage maps) is the primary differentiator vs. mechanical-only alternatives
jobSteps.rawStatement: Track real-time device health -- battery levels, wireless signal integrity, alarm frequency per door point
jobSteps.rationale: IoT reporting capability (battery alerts, signal health, dashboard coverage maps) is the primary differentiator vs. mechanical-only alternatives

jobSteps.description: Respond to alarms, investigate false positives, adjust device configuration, and dispatch maintenance work orders for battery replacement or fault resolution
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: FM operator response workflows are triggered by device-generated events; false positive rate and recovery time directly affect FM labor costs
jobSteps.rawStatement: Respond to alarms, investigate false positives, adjust device configuration, dispatch battery replacement
jobSteps.rationale: FM operator response workflows are triggered by device-generated events; false positive rate and recovery time directly affect FM labor costs

jobSteps.description: Generate compliance reports, close inspection records, archive event logs, and deliver fire safety evidence certificates to clients and regulators
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Automated event log to compliance document chain is the primary FM business value of the IoT layer; manual compilation is the top underserved need in this market
jobSteps.rawStatement: Generate compliance reports, close inspection records, archive event logs, deliver evidence certificates
jobSteps.rationale: Automated event log to compliance document chain is the primary FM business value of the IoT layer; manual compilation is the top underserved need in this market

stakeholders.who: FM safety compliance engineer / compliance technician

needs.statement: Minimize installation labor-hours per door point required to deploy wireless exit monitoring devices on existing door hardware without conduit runs or frame modification
Minimize cost per door point of electrical conduit routing and cable installation required to connect hardwired exit alarm contacts to fire panels in occupied retrofit buildings

Minimize the number of wireless door monitoring devices reaching critical battery depletion without generating a preventive maintenance alert in the FM CMMS -- measured by undetected depletions per 100 devices per year

Minimize the likelihood that exit monitoring devices pass initial commissioning tests but fail to trigger an alarm under actual emergency conditions due to misconfiguration -- measured by post-commissioning alarm failure rate per 1,000 devices

Minimize the likelihood of deploying exit monitoring configurations that render escape routes non-compliant with EN 179 / EN 1125 single-motion release requirements -- measured by non-compliant configurations per 100 installations
Minimize elapsed time between an unauthorized emergency exit door activation and alert notification reaching the responsible FM monitoring operator -- measured in minutes per event

Minimize the likelihood that emergency exit alarm devices fail to trigger under zero-voltage conditions (mains outage or complete battery depletion) -- measured by alarm failure rate under power-loss test

Minimize the number of emergency exit door openings that occur without generating a monitoring alert during a standard operating period -- measured by undetected events per 100 door activations per month
Minimize variability in event reporting latency across door monitoring devices within a single client building -- measured by maximum deviation from median report time in seconds

Minimize the number of battery depletion events that reach critical failure level before triggering a preventive maintenance work order in the FM CMMS -- measured by ratio of critical-depletion incidents to total depletions

Minimize time to restore exit monitoring coverage at a door point following device fault, wireless disconnection, or battery depletion -- measured in hours from failure detection to restored monitoring coverage

Minimize the false positive alarm rate from exit monitoring devices -- unauthorized activation alerts triggered by door vibration, accidental contact, or signal interference -- measured by false positives per 100 logged alarm events
Minimize FM labor-hours required to compile emergency exit door event logs into audit-ready fire safety inspection certificates -- measured in hours per compliance report per building

Minimize the likelihood that emergency exit event logs contain gaps or discontinuities that cause fire safety compliance documentation to fail regulatory audit -- measured by audit failure rate per 100 compliance submissions
Increase the ability to generate real-time portfolio-level compliance dashboards showing monitoring health across all client exit door points with data latency under 24 hours
Minimize time required to generate a complete inventory of emergency exit door points requiring monitoring across a new multi-building client portfolio -- measured in hours per 100 doors scoped

Minimize the total cost-per-door-per-year of emergency exit monitoring across portfolios of 50+ client buildings -- measured in EUR per door per year including hardware, installation, and ongoing maintenance

Minimize the likelihood of deploying wireless exit monitoring devices that require proprietary gateways incompatible with the client building's existing BMS protocol -- measured by BMS integration rework rate per 100 deployments

Minimize evaluation time for FM procurement teams to qualify wireless exit monitoring hardware for multi-client portfolio standardization -- measured in days from vendor shortlist to approved FM specification

Maximize completeness and tamper-evidence of emergency exit event logs that FM compliance engineers can present as primary evidence during unannounced fire safety inspections -- without manual data aggregation

Minimize manual post-processing effort required to convert exit monitoring event data into client-facing compliance reports demonstrating FM operational excellence -- measured in hours per monthly client report
Minimize FM compliance technicians' exposure to undiscovered monitoring coverage gaps across distributed client sites that could result in unrecorded incidents prior to fire safety inspection
